summary: NinjaClick is a SaaS visitor-intelligence platform: a single line of JavaScript identifies up to 80% of a website's anonymous visitors — surfacing company details and decision-maker contact info without forms or cookies. It serves B2B marketing, demand-gen, and sales teams (plus DTC brands) who want to turn anonymous, high-intent traffic into a contactable pipeline. Sold as a self-serve monthly subscription with a permanent free tier and a 14-day no-card trial, tiered by monthly identity volume.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Drive activation — get the new trial user to install the JS snippet and see their first batch of identified visitors (the aha moment) inside the 14-day window.
campaigns.trigger: Trial signup / account created — send within seconds.
campaigns.segment: New trial signups who have not yet installed the snippet; branch by role/use-case if captured at signup (sales vs. demand-gen vs. ecommerce).
campaigns.rationale: NinjaClick's entire value is visceral and instant — but only after the snippet is live. Users who never install never see identified visitors and churn immediately, so the highest-leverage email moves them to install on day 0.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, set the one goal of the trial, and deliver the single fastest first win: paste the snippet so identified visitors start appearing.
emails.subject: Your snippet's ready — see who's on your site today
emails.preview: One line of JavaScript. First identified visitors in minutes.
keyPoints: One job for today: paste your unique JS snippet into your site — copy/paste or send the 2-line guide to your developer
What happens next: real names, companies, and job titles of your visitors start landing in your dashboard within minutes to a few hours
Reassure on the #1 objection up front — GDPR-compliant, consent-based identification, no covert tracking
Human reply-to: stuck on install? Reply and a real person helps you get live before the trial clock runs

campaigns.name: Trial-to-Paid Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert activated trial users into a paid Growth/Scale plan before the 14-day window closes, and prevent loss of the leads they've already identified.
campaigns.trigger: Trial expiry − 3 days, running in parallel with usage milestones; branch on whether they activated (saw identified visitors) or stalled.
campaigns.segment: Active trials approaching expiry; high-intent branch = users with identified visitors in the dashboard, save branch = installed-but-stalled.
campaigns.rationale: Trial-to-paid is the core revenue metric for a self-serve SaaS with a hard 14-day window. The expiry email tied to the user's own identified-visitor count is the most persuasive send NinjaClick can make.

emails.purpose: Expiry warning anchored to the user's real results — quantify the visitors they identified and frame upgrading as keeping that pipeline alive.
emails.subject: Your trial ends Friday — keep the {count} leads you identified
emails.preview: Don't lose access to the companies already on your site.
emails.cta: Pick your plan & keep your data
keyPoints: Lead with their own numbers: '{count} visitors identified across {company_count} companies in 14 days' — proof the match rate is real for their traffic
Make the loss concrete — when the trial ends, identification pauses and new high-intent visitors go back to anonymous
Right-size the plan: Growth $97/mo to start, Scale $497/mo at 10,000 identities; no contract, cancel anytime
Offer a human path for Enterprise volume or integration questions — book a call instead of guessing

campaigns.name: Free-Tier Upgrade Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move permanent free-tier users (100 identities/mo) toward sales-readiness and a paid upgrade over a longer cycle by proving ROI use cases.
campaigns.trigger: Free-tier signup / free-tier user below the upgrade-intent threshold; branch by primary use case (ABM, outbound sales, ecommerce recovery).
campaigns.segment: Active free-tier accounts not yet on a paid plan; lead-score on identity-cap hits, dashboard logins, and pricing-page visits.
campaigns.rationale: The permanent free tier is NinjaClick's top-of-funnel — a large pool of installed-but-unpaid users who need stage-matched education to see why 100 identities/mo isn't enough and how peers turn identities into pipeline.

emails.purpose: Frame the real problem behind their free-tier usage — they're identifying intent they can't fully act on — and teach the workflow that turns identified visitors into booked meetings.
emails.subject: You're identifying visitors — here's how to turn them into pipeline
emails.preview: The 3-step play SDR teams run on NinjaClick data every morning.
keyPoints: Reframe: identifying visitors is step one — the ROI comes from routing them into outbound/ABM workflows fast
Teach the framework: every morning, prioritize the accounts that visited high-intent pages, enrich, and reach out before they go cold
Show the ceiling gently — at 100 identities/mo most teams hit the cap by mid-month and miss real buyers
Soft path to value: a short case angle ('how a demand-gen team turned 3x more traffic into conversations') with a link, not a hard sell

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate lapsed free-tier users and churned subscribers — especially those who signed up but never installed the snippet — and clean the list to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: Inactivity threshold (60–90 days no logins/installs) or subscription cancellation; branch dormant-never-activated vs. churned-paid.
campaigns.segment: Dormant accounts that never saw an identified visitor (re-onboard), and churned paying customers (what's changed since you left).
campaigns.rationale: A large share of NinjaClick's drop-off is install-and-abandon — users who never reached the aha moment. A targeted win-back that removes the install blocker recovers accounts cheaply and keeps sender reputation healthy.

emails.purpose: Pattern-interrupt for never-activated users: remind them they're still leaving money on the table and make re-installing frictionless.
emails.subject: You never saw who's on your site — let's fix that in 2 minutes
emails.preview: Your snippet's still waiting. Identified visitors are one paste away.
keyPoints: Name the gap plainly — they signed up but never installed, so they never saw the names, companies, and contacts they could be reaching
Remove the blocker: pre-filled snippet, a copy-paste guide, and a 'send to my developer' option
Restate the stakes — 95%+ of their traffic is still leaving anonymous every day it's not running
Low-pressure human offer: reply and we'll get you live, or tell us what stopped you

campaigns.name: Value Newsletter
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind and build authority across the long cycle — deepen engagement and create natural expansion conversations without discounting.
campaigns.trigger: Scheduled cadence (biweekly); suppress active onboarding and dormant accounts (routed to win-back).
campaigns.segment: All engaged free and paid contacts; segment editions by persona — sales/SDR, demand-gen/ABM, ecommerce/DTC.
campaigns.rationale: NinjaClick's audience lives on intent data and prospecting tactics. A use-case-led newsletter keeps installed users active, reinforces ROI to reduce churn, and surfaces the heavier plans as identity needs grow.

emails.purpose: Hero edition establishing the recurring value format — one sharp, actionable idea on turning visitor intelligence into pipeline.
emails.subject: The intent signal most teams ignore (it's already in your dashboard)
emails.preview: 3 ways to act on identified visitors this week.
keyPoints: One useful idea per send: this edition = the highest-intent page visits to watch and how to route them to sales same-day
80/20 value-to-offer — teach the play, then a single soft pointer to the relevant feature or plan
Recurring habit section ('This week's intent play') so readers know what to expect each edition
Reinforce privacy/compliance positioning so the data-sensitive audience stays confident using the product
